1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a tumor (neoplasm)?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A tumor is an abnormal clonal proliferation of cells.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which feature best describes a benign tumor?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Benign tumors are localized, well differentiated and non-invasive.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which feature best describes a malignant tumor?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Malignant tumors are invasive, poorly differentiated and capable of metastasis.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is metastasis?
a: Spread of malignant cells to distant sites
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Metastasis is the spread of malignant cells to distant sites.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is hyperplasia?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Hyperplasia is an increase in the number of cells.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is hypertrophy?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Hypertrophy is an increase in the size of cells.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is neoplasia?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Neoplasia is autonomous, uncontrolled and disorganized growth of cells.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Goiter results from which combination?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Goiter is caused by hyperplasia and hypertrophy of thyroid follicular cells.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What are the two major components of a tumor?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Tumors consist of parenchyma and stroma.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which component of a tumor determines its behavior and classification?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Parenchyma determines tumor behavior and classification.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is the function of tumor stroma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Stroma provides blood supply and structural support.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: The suffix '-oma' usually indicates what type of tumor?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: The suffix '-oma' usually indicates a benign tumor.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is a malignant exception to the '-oma' rule?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Melanoma is malignant despite ending in '-oma'.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is another malignant exception to the '-oma' rule?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Lymphoma is malignant despite ending in '-oma'.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is an adenoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: An adenoma is a benign epithelial tumor arising from glandular tissue.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a papilloma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Papilloma is a benign epithelial tumor with finger-like projections.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a cystadenoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Cystadenoma is a benign cystic epithelial tumor.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a carcinoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Carcinoma is a malignant tumor of epithelial origin.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is an example of carcinoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Squamous cell carcinoma is a malignant epithelial tumor.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a sarcoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Sarcoma is a malignant tumor of mesenchymal origin.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is an example of a sarcoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Fibrosarcoma is a malignant tumor of mesenchymal origin.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is another example of a sarcoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Liposarcoma is a malignant tumor arising from adipose tissue.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Osteosarcoma is a malignant tumor derived from which tissue?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Osteosarcoma is a malignant tumor of bone.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Chondrosarcoma is a malignant tumor derived from which tissue?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Chondrosarcoma is a malignant tumor of cartilage.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is a mesenchymal tissue?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Bone is a mesenchymal tissue.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which of the following is NOT a mesenchymal tissue?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Thyroid epithelium is epithelial, not mesenchymal.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a pleomorphic adenoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Pleomorphic adenoma is a benign salivary gland mixed tumor.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Pleomorphic adenoma contains which elements?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Pleomorphic adenoma contains epithelial and mesenchymal-like elements.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is the malignant counterpart of pleomorphic adenoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma is the malignant mixed tumor.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a teratoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Teratomas contain tissues derived from more than one germ layer.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is another name for a mature cystic teratoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A mature cystic teratoma is also called a dermoid cyst.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which feature best describes a mature cystic teratoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Mature cystic teratomas are benign and well differentiated.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which type of teratoma is malignant?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Immature teratomas are malignant and contain immature tissues.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Having more than 50 melanocytic nevi increases the risk of which malignancy?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: More than 50 melanocytic nevi increase the risk of melanoma.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a primary tumor?
a: Tumor originating in the organ where it is found
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A primary tumor originates in the organ where it is found.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a secondary tumor?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A secondary tumor is a metastatic tumor from another site.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a hamartoma?
a: Disorganized mature tissue native to the organ
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Hamartoma is normal tissue in an abnormal arrangement.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which phrase best describes a hamartoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Hamartoma is normal tissue in an abnormal arrangement.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Pulmonary hamartoma classically appears as what on chest X-ray?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Pulmonary hamartoma commonly presents as a coin lesion.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which tissues are commonly found in a pulmonary hamartoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Pulmonary hamartomas contain cartilage, fat and connective tissue.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a choristoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A choristoma is normal tissue in an abnormal (ectopic) location.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which phrase best describes a choristoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Choristoma is normal tissue in an abnormal location.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which is an example of a choristoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Pancreatic tissue in the stomach wall is a classic choristoma.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a Ghon focus?
a: Primary subpleural lung lesion of tuberculosis
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A Ghon focus is the primary subpleural lesion of tuberculosis.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a Ghon complex?
a: Ghon focus plus hilar lymph node involvement
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A Ghon complex consists of a Ghon focus and hilar lymph node involvement.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a granuloma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: A granuloma is a collection of activated macrophages (epithelioid histiocytes).

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which cytokine activates macrophages and promotes granuloma formation?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Interferon-γ activates macrophages and promotes granuloma formation.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is multinodular goiter (MNG)?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Multinodular goiter is a hyperplastic enlargement of the thyroid gland.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which benign lesion is a common differential diagnosis of a coin lesion in the lung?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Pulmonary hamartoma is a common cause of a coin lesion on chest X-ray.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a Peutz-Jeghers polyp?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Peutz-Jeghers syndrome is associated with hamartomatous polyps.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is a hemangioma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Hemangioma is a benign proliferation of blood vessels.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Trophoblastic tumors arise from which tissue?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Trophoblastic tumors arise from placental epithelium.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is the benign counterpart of hepatocellular carcinoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: The benign counterpart of hepatocellular carcinoma is hepatic adenoma.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: A malignant epithelial tumor is called what?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Malignant epithelial tumors are called carcinomas.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: A malignant mesenchymal tumor is called what?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Malignant mesenchymal tumors are called sarcomas.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: The prefix 'Adeno-' refers to which structure?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Adeno refers to glandular tissue.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Papilloma is characterized by what microscopic appearance?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Papillomas have finger-like epithelial projections.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which phrase best describes a hamartoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Hamartoma is normal tissue in an abnormal arrangement.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Which phrase best describes a choristoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Choristoma is normal tissue in an abnormal location.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: What is the defining feature of a teratoma?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Teratomas contain tissues derived from multiple germ layers.

1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.q: Goiter is best described as which combination?
1_neoplasia_tumor_naming.l: Goiter results from both hyperplasia and hypertrophy.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is metastasis?
a: Spread of tumor cells to a distant site
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastasis is the spread of tumor cells from the primary site to a distant site.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most important feature distinguishing benign from malignant tumors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastasis is the most important feature distinguishing benign from malignant tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are metastatic implants called?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic implants are called metastases (mets).

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How does tumor size relate to metastasis risk?
a: Larger aggressive tumors have higher risk
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The larger and more aggressive the tumor, the greater the risk of metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Approximately what percentage of newly diagnosed malignant tumors already have metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: About 50% of newly diagnosed malignant tumors already have metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Metastasis may occur through which routes?
a: Lymphatics, blood vessels, and body cavities
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastasis occurs through lymphatics, blood vessels, or body cavities.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How many major routes of metastasis exist?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Three routes of metastasis are lymphatic, hematogenous, and transcoelomic spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which route is most common for carcinoma metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lymphatic spread is the most common route for carcinomas.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which route is most common for sarcoma metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Hematogenous spread is the most common route for sarcomas.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which thyroid carcinoma commonly spreads through blood vessels?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Follicular thyroid carcinoma is an exception that spreads via blood.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which kidney cancer commonly spreads through blood vessels?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Renal cell carcinoma commonly spreads through blood vessels.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which liver cancer frequently spreads hematogenously?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Hepatocellular carcinoma frequently spreads hematogenously.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are regional lymph nodes considered in cancer spread?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Regional lymph nodes are the first line of defense against cancer spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How are metastatic lymph nodes typically described?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic lymph nodes are enlarged, hard, and non-tender.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Lymphatic spread generally follows what pattern?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lymphatic spread follows normal lymphatic drainage pathways.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Breast carcinoma commonly spreads to which lymph nodes?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Breast carcinoma commonly spreads to axillary lymph nodes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Lung carcinoma commonly spreads to which lymph nodes?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lung carcinoma commonly spreads to bronchial and tracheal lymph nodes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which vessels are invaded more easily during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Veins are invaded more easily than arteries during metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Why do arteries resist tumor invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Arteries resist invasion because of their thick walls.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumors entering the portal circulation commonly metastasize to which organ?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumors entering the portal circulation commonly metastasize to the liver.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: GI tumors commonly metastasize to which organ through the portal vein?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: GI tumors commonly metastasize to the liver through the portal vein.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumors entering systemic veins most commonly metastasize to which organ?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumors entering systemic veins commonly metastasize to the lungs.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which is more common: primary lung tumors or lung metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lung metastases are more common than primary lung tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which is a common hematogenous metastatic site?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Common hematogenous metastatic sites include liver, lung, bone, brain, and adrenal glands.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Renal cell carcinoma commonly invades which vessel and major vein?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Renal cell carcinoma can invade the renal vein and IVC.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Hepatocellular carcinoma commonly invades which veins?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Hepatocellular carcinoma can invade portal and hepatic veins.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Seeding of body cavities occurs when malignant cells do what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Seeding occurs when malignant cells exfoliate and implant on cavity surfaces.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most common site of transcoelomic spread?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The peritoneal cavity is the most common site of transcoelomic spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cancers commonly seed the peritoneum?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Ovarian, GI, and pancreatic cancers commonly seed the peritoneum.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cavity may be seeded by lung cancers?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lung cancers may seed the pleural cavity.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Glioblastoma may spread through which space?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Glioblastoma may spread through the subarachnoid space.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is a Krukenberg tumor?
a: Metastatic ovarian tumor with signet-ring cells
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Krukenberg tumor is a metastatic ovarian tumor containing signet-ring cells.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Krukenberg tumors most commonly originate from which cancer?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Krukenberg tumors most commonly originate from gastric carcinoma.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the likely mechanism of spread in Krukenberg tumors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Retrograde lymphatic spread is the likely mechanism of Krukenberg tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most common site of bone metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The vertebral column is the most common site of bone metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which venous plexus explains vertebral metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Batson paravertebral venous plexus explains vertebral metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the second most common site of bone metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The proximal femur is the second most common site of bone metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most common symptom of bone metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Pain is the most common symptom of bone metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the best treatment for painful bone metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Local radiation therapy is the best treatment for painful bone metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Bone metastases are classified into which two major types?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Bone metastases are classified as osteoblastic or osteolytic.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Osteoblastic metastases cause what change in bone?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteoblastic metastases produce increased bone formation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cancer commonly causes osteoblastic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Prostate carcinoma commonly causes osteoblastic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How do osteoblastic metastases appear on X-ray?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteoblastic metastases appear as radiodense lesions on X-ray.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which laboratory value is increased in osteoblastic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteoblastic metastases increase serum alkaline phosphatase.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Osteolytic metastases produce what effect on bone?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteolytic metastases destroy bone and create radiolucent lesions.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cancer commonly produces osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Renal cell carcinoma commonly produces osteolytic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which mediators activate osteoclasts in osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteolytic metastases activate osteoclasts through PGE₂ and IL-1.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What fracture type is common in osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Pathological fractures are common in osteolytic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Bone metastasis is the most common cause of what condition in elderly patients with bone pain?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Bone metastasis is the most common cause of hypercalcemia with bone pain in the elderly.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Hypercalcemia from bone metastasis may cause which complication?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Hypercalcemia may cause renal stones, gastric ulcers, pancreatitis, and metastatic calcification.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which adhesion molecule is most commonly lost during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: E-cadherin is the major cell adhesion molecule lost in metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Loss of E-cadherin allows tumor cells to do what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Loss of E-cadherin allows tumor cells to detach from one another.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: E-cadherin function is lost in most cancers of which origin?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: E-cadherin function is lost in most epithelial cancers.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which transcription factors suppress E-cadherin expression?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: SNAIL and TWIST suppress E-cadherin expression.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: SNAIL and TWIST promote which process?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: SNAIL and TWIST promote epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT).

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What does EMT stand for?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT stands for epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: EMT is essential for which tumor property?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT is essential for tumor metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: EMT downregulates which epithelial marker?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT downregulates epithelial markers such as E-cadherin.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: EMT upregulates which mesenchymal marker?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT upregulates mesenchymal markers such as vimentin.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which enzyme degrades the basement membrane during tumor invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Type IV collagenase degrades basement membrane.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are the key enzymes involved in tumor invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) are key enzymes in tumor invasion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: MMP-9 belongs to which enzyme group?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: MMP-9 is a type IV collagenase (gelatinase).

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: MMP-9 releases which growth factor trapped in the extracellular matrix?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: MMP-9 releases VEGF trapped within the extracellular matrix.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells attach to which basement membrane protein?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells attach to laminin in the basement membrane.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells attach to which extracellular matrix protein?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells attach to fibronectin in the extracellular matrix.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which tissues are relatively resistant to tumor invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Cartilage and elastic tissue are relatively resistant to tumor invasion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor migration may occur through what type of movement?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor migration can occur through ameboid movement without matrix degradation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor-derived cytokines primarily stimulate what process?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor-derived cytokines stimulate tumor cell locomotion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells in circulation are vulnerable to what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells are vulnerable to immune destruction while in circulation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells often travel in circulation coated with which cells?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells often travel as emboli coated with platelets and leukocytes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Overexpression of which molecule favors metastatic spread?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: CD44 overexpression favors metastatic spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Most metastases occur in which location relative to circulation?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Most metastases occur in the first capillary bed encountered.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Breast cancer commonly expresses which chemokine receptors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Breast cancer commonly expresses CXCR4 and CCR7 chemokine receptors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which chemokines help direct breast cancer metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: CXCL12 and CCL21 help direct breast cancer metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor homing depends on which factors?
a: Adhesion molecules, chemokines and organ microenvironment
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor homing depends on adhesion molecules, chemokines and organ microenvironment.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What do metastatic suppressor genes do?
a: Inhibit metastasis without affecting the primary tumor
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic suppressor genes inhibit metastasis without affecting the primary tumor.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which microRNAs are examples of metastatic suppressor genes?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: miR-335 and miR-126 are metastatic suppressor genes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What do metastatic oncogenes do?
a: Promote metastasis without affecting the primary tumor
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic oncogenes promote metastasis without affecting the primary tumor.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which microRNA is a metastatic oncogene?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: miR-10b is a metastatic oncogene.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which transcription factors are considered metastasis-promoting oncogenes?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: SNAIL and TWIST are metastasis-promoting oncogenes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor emboli form by aggregation with which cells?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor emboli form by aggregation with platelets and leukocytes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the first step in the mechanism of metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Detachment of tumor cells is the first step in metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the second step in the mechanism of metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Basement membrane degradation follows detachment.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows basement membrane degradation in metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells next attach to extracellular matrix components.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which step comes after attachment during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Migration follows attachment in the metastatic sequence.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which step follows migration during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells invade blood vessels after migration.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What occurs after vascular invasion in metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells enter and travel through the circulation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which step occurs after circulation during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Homing to a distant organ follows circulation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the final step of metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Formation of a secondary tumor is the final step of metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which sequence correctly describes metastasis?
a: Detachment → Basement membrane degradation → Attachment → Migration → Vascular invasion → Circulation → Homing → Secondary tumor formation
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The metastatic cascade follows a specific sequential pathway from detachment to secondary tumor formation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Malignant tumors typically show what degree of differentiation?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Malignant tumors commonly show poor differentiation and anaplasia.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is anaplasia?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Anaplasia refers to loss of differentiation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Differentiation refers to what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Differentiation is the resemblance of tumor cells to normal parent tissue.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Benign tumors are usually how differentiated?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Benign tumors are well differentiated and resemble host tissue.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Abundant atypical mitoses suggest what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Abundant atypical mitoses are a feature of malignant tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor giant cells are most commonly associated with which type of tumor?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor giant cells are commonly seen in malignant tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Cancer cells may demonstrate which abnormal mitotic pattern?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Cancer cells often show tripolar or multipolar mitotic spindles.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Benign tumors are typically described as what?
a: Slow growing, well demarcated and non-invasive
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Benign tumors are slow growing, well demarcated and non-invasive.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Benign tumors are usually surrounded by what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Benign tumors are usually encapsulated.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is dysplasia?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Dysplasia is a reversible pre-neoplastic lesion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cancer commonly causes osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Renal cell carcinoma commonly causes osteolytic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How do osteolytic metastases appear on X-ray?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteolytic metastases produce radiolucent lesions.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which mediators activate osteoclasts in osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteolytic metastases activate osteoclasts through PGE₂ and IL-1.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What complication is common in osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Pathological fractures are common in osteolytic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Bone metastasis is the most common cause of what condition in elderly patients with bone pain?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Bone metastasis is the most common cause of hypercalcemia with bone pain in the elderly.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Hypercalcemia from bone metastasis may cause which complication?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Hypercalcemia may cause renal stones, gastric ulcers, pancreatitis, and metastatic calcification.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which adhesion molecule is most commonly lost during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: E-cadherin is the major adhesion molecule lost during metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Loss of E-cadherin allows tumor cells to do what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Loss of E-cadherin allows tumor cells to detach from each other.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Loss of E-cadherin function is seen in most cancers of which origin?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: E-cadherin function is lost in most epithelial cancers.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which transcription factors suppress E-cadherin expression?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: SNAIL and TWIST suppress E-cadherin expression.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: SNAIL and TWIST promote which process?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: SNAIL and TWIST promote epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT).

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: EMT is essential for which process?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT is essential for metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which epithelial marker is downregulated during EMT?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT downregulates epithelial markers such as E-cadherin.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which mesenchymal marker is upregulated during EMT?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: EMT upregulates mesenchymal markers such as vimentin.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which enzyme degrades the basement membrane during invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Type IV collagenase degrades the basement membrane.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are the key enzymes involved in tumor invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Matrix metalloproteinases are key enzymes in tumor invasion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: MMP-9 belongs to which enzyme group?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: MMP-9 is a type IV collagenase known as gelatinase.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: MMP-9 releases which growth factor trapped in the extracellular matrix?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: MMP-9 releases VEGF trapped within the extracellular matrix.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells attach to which basement membrane protein?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells attach to laminin in the basement membrane.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells attach to which extracellular matrix protein?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells attach to fibronectin in the extracellular matrix.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which tissues are relatively resistant to tumor invasion?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Cartilage and elastic tissue are relatively resistant to tumor invasion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor migration can occur through what type of movement?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor migration can occur through ameboid movement without matrix degradation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor-derived cytokines stimulate which process?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor-derived cytokines stimulate tumor cell locomotion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells in circulation are particularly vulnerable to what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells are vulnerable to immune destruction while in circulation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor cells often travel in circulation coated with what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells often travel as emboli coated with platelets and leukocytes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Overexpression of which molecule favors metastatic spread?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: CD44 overexpression favors metastatic spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Most metastases occur in which location?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Most metastases occur in the first capillary bed encountered.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Breast cancer commonly expresses which chemokine receptors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Breast cancer commonly expresses CXCR4 and CCR7 chemokine receptors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which chemokines help direct breast cancer metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: CXCL12 and CCL21 help direct breast cancer metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor homing depends on which factors?
a: Adhesion molecules, chemokines and organ microenvironment
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor homing depends on adhesion molecules, chemokines and organ microenvironment.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What do metastatic suppressor genes do?
a: Inhibit metastasis without affecting the primary tumor
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic suppressor genes inhibit metastasis without affecting the primary tumor.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which microRNAs are metastatic suppressor genes?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: miR-335 and miR-126 are metastatic suppressor genes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What do metastatic oncogenes do?
a: Promote metastasis without affecting the primary tumor
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic oncogenes promote metastasis without affecting the primary tumor.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which microRNA is a metastatic oncogene?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: miR-10b is a metastatic oncogene.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which transcription factors are metastasis-promoting oncogenes?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: SNAIL and TWIST are metastasis-promoting oncogenes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor emboli form by aggregation with which cells?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor emboli form by aggregation with platelets and leukocytes.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the first step in metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Detachment of tumor cells is the first step in metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows detachment in the metastatic cascade?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Basement membrane degradation follows detachment.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows basement membrane degradation in metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells next attach to extracellular matrix components.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows attachment during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Migration follows attachment in the metastatic sequence.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows migration during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells invade blood vessels after migration.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows vascular invasion in metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor cells enter and travel through the circulation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What follows circulation during metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Homing to a distant organ follows circulation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the final step of metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Formation of a secondary tumor is the final step of metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which sequence correctly describes the metastatic cascade?
a: Detachment → Basement membrane degradation → Attachment → Migration → Vascular invasion → Circulation → Homing → Secondary tumor formation
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The metastatic cascade follows a specific sequence from detachment to secondary tumor formation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Malignant tumors typically show what degree of differentiation?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Malignant tumors commonly show poor differentiation and anaplasia.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is anaplasia?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Anaplasia refers to lack of differentiation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is differentiation?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Differentiation is the resemblance of tumor cells to normal parent tissue.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How are benign tumors usually differentiated?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Benign tumors are well differentiated and resemble host tissue.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Abundant atypical mitoses are suggestive of what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Abundant atypical mitoses suggest malignancy.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Tumor giant cells are commonly seen in which type of tumor?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor giant cells are commonly seen in malignant tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Cancer cells may demonstrate which abnormal mitotic pattern?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Cancer cells may show tripolar or multipolar mitotic spindles.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Benign tumors are typically described as what?
a: Slow growing, well demarcated and non-invasive
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Benign tumors are slow growing, well demarcated and non-invasive.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Benign tumors are usually surrounded by what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Benign tumors are usually encapsulated.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is dysplasia?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Dysplasia is a pre-neoplastic lesion and is reversible.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Is dysplasia reversible or irreversible?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Dysplasia is a reversible lesion.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is carcinoma in situ?
a: Severe dysplasia involving full epithelial thickness
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Carcinoma in situ is severe dysplasia involving the full epithelial thickness.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What layer involvement characterizes carcinoma in situ?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Carcinoma in situ involves the full thickness of the epithelium.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most important feature distinguishing benign from malignant tumors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastasis is the most important feature distinguishing benign from malignant tumors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is metastasis?
a: Spread of tumor cells to distant sites
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastasis is the spread of tumor cells to distant sites.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Lymphatic spread is the most common route of metastasis for which tumors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lymphatic spread is the most common route of metastasis for carcinomas.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Lymph nodes serve as what in cancer spread?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lymph nodes are the first line of defense against cancer spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Strong lymph node immune responses may have what effect?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Strong lymph node immune responses may improve survival.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How are metastatic lymph nodes typically described?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic lymph nodes are enlarged, hard, and non-tender.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Lymphomas classically present as what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Lymphomas classically present as painless lymphadenopathy.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Rhabdomyosarcoma is a malignant tumor of which tissue?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Rhabdomyosarcoma is a malignant tumor of skeletal muscle.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Leiomyosarcoma is a malignant tumor of which tissue?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Leiomyosarcoma is a malignant tumor of smooth muscle.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which carcinoma commonly invades the renal vein and IVC?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Renal cell carcinoma commonly invades the renal vein and IVC.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which organs are common blood-borne metastatic sites?
a: Liver, lung, bone, brain, adrenal glands
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Major blood-borne metastatic sites are liver, lung, bone, brain, and adrenal glands.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are tumor emboli?
a: Clusters of malignant cells circulating in blood vessels
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor emboli are clusters of malignant cells circulating in blood vessels.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Krukenberg tumor is a metastatic tumor involving which organ?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Krukenberg tumor is a metastatic ovarian tumor with signet-ring cells.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cells characterize a Krukenberg tumor?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Krukenberg tumors contain signet-ring cells.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most common primary site of a Krukenberg tumor?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The stomach is the most common primary site of a Krukenberg tumor.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are the three major routes of metastasis?
a: Lymphatic, hematogenous, seeding of body cavities
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Routes of metastasis are lymphatic spread, hematogenous spread, and seeding of body cavities.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which route is most commonly used by carcinomas?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Carcinomas usually spread through lymphatics.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which route is most commonly used by sarcomas?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Sarcomas usually spread through blood vessels.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which vessels are the principal route of hematogenous spread?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Veins are the principal route of hematogenous spread.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Why are arteries less commonly invaded by tumors?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Arteries are less commonly invaded because of their thicker walls.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Metastases are usually what?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastases are usually multiple rather than solitary.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What are cannonball lesions?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Cannonball lesions are multiple rounded pulmonary metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most common site of bone metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The vertebral column is the most common site of bone metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which venous plexus explains vertebral metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Batson paravertebral venous plexus explains vertebral metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the second most common site of bone metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: The proximal femur is the second most common site of bone metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the most common symptom of bone metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Pain is the most common symptom of bone metastasis.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What is the best treatment for painful bone metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Local radiation therapy is the best treatment for painful bone metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Bone metastases are classified into which two categories?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Bone metastases are classified as osteoblastic or osteolytic.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What do osteoblastic metastases cause?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteoblastic metastases produce increased bone formation.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cancer commonly causes osteoblastic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Prostate carcinoma commonly causes osteoblastic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: How do osteoblastic metastases appear on X-ray?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteoblastic metastases appear as radiodense lesions on X-ray.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which laboratory value increases in osteoblastic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteoblastic metastases increase serum alkaline phosphatase.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What do osteolytic metastases cause?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteolytic metastases destroy bone and create radiolucent lesions.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which cancer commonly produces osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Renal cell carcinoma commonly produces osteolytic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which mediators activate osteoclasts in osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Osteolytic metastases activate osteoclasts through PGE₂ and IL-1.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: What fracture type is common in osteolytic metastases?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Pathological fractures are common in osteolytic metastases.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Bone metastasis is the most common cause of what condition in elderly patients with bone pain?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Bone metastasis is the most common cause of hypercalcemia with bone pain in the elderly.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Hypercalcemia from bone metastasis may cause which complication?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Hypercalcemia may cause renal stones, gastric ulcers, pancreatitis, and metastatic calcification.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Breast cancer commonly expresses CXCR4 and CCR7 chemokine receptors.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.q: Which chemokines help direct breast cancer metastasis?
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: CXCL12 and CCL21 help direct breast cancer metastasis.

a: Adhesion molecules, chemokines and organ microenvironment
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Tumor homing depends on adhesion molecules, chemokines and organ microenvironment.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic suppressor genes inhibit metastasis without affecting the primary tumor.

2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: miR-335 and miR-126 are metastatic suppressor genes.

a: Promote metastasis without affecting the primary tumor
2_neoplasia_cancer_metastasis.l: Metastatic oncogenes promote metastasis without affecting the primary tumor.
